other_observers: none; I found and photographed this bird
duration: on and off for about a minute
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch just west southwest of the Banding Station.
description: This was a small skulking bird of dense brushy habitat on Chico Basin Ranch. When I first saw it I thought it was probably a warbler. The bird was yellowish underneath with a dull whitish throat. The face was gray green with what appeared as an upper and lower eye arc but photos show it has a faint spectacle. The bill was gray with a hooked tip as in vireo, not warbler. The wings were gray edged in yellow. There were two whitish wingbars but the upper one was mostly concealed. The upperparts were dull greenish changing to green gray on the head. The tail seemed short for a vireo and the undertail coverts had a light yellow white cast.Behaviors: Skulked around at head height in a Russian olive thicket. Grabbed a small green caterpillar. Always had parts of its body hidden behind low twigs.Call: none heardPlumage: unknown - fresh
